cot_prefix: Okay, let's try to figure out the best title for this passage. The passage is talking about research from Edinburgh University that suggests our genes play a bigger role than upbringing or environment in determining traits like determination, sociability, self-control, and sense of purpose. These traits are linked to success or failure. They used twins to study nature vs. nurture. The conclusion seems to be that DNA has a significant influence on these personality aspects, which in turn affect success. But the professor also mentions that even if your genes aren't perfect, having a sense of purpose is key. Looking at the options: A) How do people work hard? – The passage isn't about the methods people use to work hard, but rather about why some people have traits that lead to success. So probably not A. B) Why can some people succeed? – This
